title: Stale booking state contamination — new booking request inherits previous confirmed time
failing_behavior: After a booking completes (e.g., Pedicure/Helen/4:15 PM), the customer starts a fresh session or conversation turn and says 'I like to book a pedicure today at 3PM with Helen.' The AI immediately books Pedicure/Helen/4:15 PM instead of 3:00 PM. The system never asks for contact info — it reuses stale name/phone from the previous booking — and writes the wrong time to Firestore.
root_cause: Chain of three compounding issues: (1) _submitDirectBooking restores name/phone/services/staff to biz._bookingState after booking completes (required invariant for cancel/modify). (2) _buildBookingStateContext emits 'BOOKING STATUS: CONFIRMED' when _lastBookingId && s.name && s.phone && !s.date && !s.time — this signal was present indefinitely after booking. (3) When Claude receives a new booking request after seeing CONFIRMED signal, it sets pendingAction=modify_booking. The isModify=true flag causes the ESCALATE path to set existingBookingId = biz._lastBookingId and call _submitDirectBooking directly, skipping contact-info collection and using the stale 4:15 PM time from the prior ESCALATE STATE rather than the freshly requested 3:00 PM.
fix_summary: Five-part fix: (1) _submitDirectBooking stores biz._lastConfirmedDate and biz._lastConfirmedTime after each confirmed booking. (2) CONFIRMED signal now includes the confirmed slot time and an explicit instruction not to reuse it for new requests. (3) biz._prevPendingAction captured before the Claude API call so it is accessible in the async send() callback. (4) isModify detection in _handleMessage gains a stale-time guard: when modify_booking came from this turn's STATE only (not prior turn, not existingBookingId in STATE, not a cross-session lookup) AND the booking time matches _lastConfirmedTime, isModify is cleared. (5) Same guard applied in the early-check path (_inModify in send()). (6) _mergeState clears _lastBookingId/_lastConfirmedTime/_lastConfirmedDate when a fresh booking_request with a different time arrives.

conversation.content: [Prior booking already completed in same session: Pedicure/Helen/2026-04-09/16:15, biz._lastBookingId='abc123', biz._lastConfirmedTime='16:15', biz._bookingState.name='JD', biz._bookingState.phone='4084397522']
I like to book a pedicure today at 3PM with Helen. My phone is 4084397522 and I am JD

I'd love to book that for you! Let me check Helen's availability at 3:00 PM today.
[STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":["Pedicure"],"staff":"Helen","date":"2026-04-09","time":"15:00","name":"JD","phone":"4084397522","lang":"en","pendingAction":"booking_offer","existingBookingId":null}]
conversation.notes: EXPECTED: AI treats this as fresh booking_request, not a modification. Proposes 3:00 PM, not 4:15 PM. pendingAction=booking_offer (not modify_booking). existingBookingId=null.

conversation.content:
Your Pedicure with Helen at 3:00 PM is confirmed and booked. We look forward to seeing you!
[STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":["Pedicure"],"staff":"Helen","date":"2026-04-09","time":"15:00","name":"JD","phone":"4084397522","lang":"en","pendingAction":null,"existingBookingId":null}]
[BOOKING:{"services":["Pedicure"],"staff":"Helen","date":"2026-04-09","time":"15:00","name":"JD","phone":"4084397522"}]
[ESCALATE:appointment]

stale_time_guard_behavior.scenario: After Pedicure/Helen/16:15 is confirmed, user requests Pedicure/Helen/15:00
stale_time_guard_behavior.without_fix: isModify=true → _submitDirectBooking called with time=16:15 → wrong booking created at 4:15 PM
stale_time_guard_behavior.with_fix_step1: _mergeState: new booking_request with time=15:00 ≠ _lastConfirmedTime=16:15 → _lastBookingId cleared → CONFIRMED signal no longer fires
stale_time_guard_behavior.with_fix_step2: Even if CONFIRMED signal fires (same-turn race): isModify guard detects _isModFromState=true && !_isModFromPrev && !_isModFromId && !_xsLookupDone AND time=15:00 ≠ 16:15 → guard does NOT trigger (different times) → fresh booking proceeds normally
stale_time_guard_behavior.same_time_edge_case: If user says 'book me at 4:15 PM again' → time=16:15 === _lastConfirmedTime → guard triggers → isModify cleared → system treats as new booking, runs full availability check
stale_time_guard_behavior.legitimate_reschedule_protection: Multi-turn reschedule: _isModFromPrev=true → guard bypassed. Cross-session reschedule: _xsLookupDone=true → guard bypassed. One-turn reschedule to new time: times differ → guard bypassed.

verify_fix_strings: do NOT reuse the confirmed slot
code_areas: nailsalon/receptionist.js:_buildBookingStateContext() — CONFIRMED signal includes slot, anti-stale instructions
nailsalon/receptionist.js:_mergeState() — clears _lastBookingId when fresh time arrives
notes: The fix is deliberately conservative: it only clears isModify when ALL of these are true: (a) modify_booking signal came only from this turn's STATE (not prior turn context, not explicit existingBookingId), (b) no cross-session lookup was done, (c) the booking time matches the last confirmed time. This prevents false negatives on legitimate reschedules while catching the exact stale-time contamination pattern.
confidence_gap: Runner verifies: all five guard strings present in source. Live testing required to confirm that a same-session reschedule immediately after a confirmation (user says 'actually change it to 5PM') still routes correctly as a modify_booking and does not incorrectly trigger the stale-time guard.
